Operation 'sasportal.nodes.devices.createSigned' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, poor_ai_interpretability. Missing summary entirely. Description is too brief and lacks critical details about inputs (SasPortalCreateSignedDeviceRequest), outputs (SasPortalDevice), and the significance of 'signed' device creation. Added context about signing capabilities and spectrum access system domain to improve AI agent understanding of when this operation should be used.

data.current_description: Creates a signed device under a node or customer.
data.summary_suggestion: Create a signed device under a node or customer
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new signed device resource under the specified node or customer parent. Requires a SasPortalCreateSignedDeviceRequest containing device configuration and signing parameters. Returns the newly created SasPortalDevice object with assigned device identifier and signed credentials. This operation provisions a device with cryptographic signing capabilities for spectrum access system operations.
data.reasoning: Missing summary entirely.
